lines: Ashes drop from Kael’thas in Tempest Keep (The Eye); instance is a weekly lockout.
The mount is a classic ultra-rare raid drop—budget many clears unless RNG smiles.
PUGs and communities sometimes organize legacy TK runs; agree on loot rules before joining.
Check your edition’s loot rules (personal vs legacy) so you know whether the mount can trade.
Invincible’s Reins drop from the Lich King in Icecrown Citadel on the legacy heroic-25 difficulty ruleset for that content.
Widely considered one of the rarest farmable mounts—expect a long campaign.
ICC has multiple wings; full clears take time; plan lockouts if you’re on multiple characters.
Before paying for a ‘mount run,’ verify difficulty, lockout, and what the group guarantees.
The Time-Lost Proto-Drake is a Storm Peaks rare spawn; competition for the tag is common.
Vyragosa shares the same general rare ecosystem—many stories are about mistiming or missing the TLPD path.
NPC-scanning addons and community spawn timers help but are not a guarantee; WoW’s API limits still apply in 12.x.
Camping can take days or weeks; set realistic expectations and take breaks.
Chestnut Mare is a vendor mount—buy from human racial vendors (e.g. Elwynn area) for a few silver.
No grind: if it’s missing from your journal, check the correct vendor list for your faction.
Useful as a cheap completion tick for mount collectors.
Wrathful Gladiator’s Frost Wyrm was a Wrath-era arena seasonal reward—not obtainable the same way today.
New players often discover it only via the journal grayed out; verify current sources in the mount panel.
If you see it on the BMAH or promotions, those windows are rare—community news threads spell out timing.
Felsteed is the Warlock class mount quest chain (Classic-era flow varies by edition—check your client).
Low friction compared to drop mounts; new warlocks usually pick it up through the class intro experience.
If the spell is missing, verify you completed the right questline for your game version.
